title: Revocation and Status Mechanism with Bitstring Status List and Bounded-Freshness Delta (Δ)
description: Define how credential status is published, distributed, verified, and cached using W3C Bitstring Status List v1.0 and a bounded-freshness guarantee parameter Δ. Define on-chain anchoring for tamper evidence, staleness policies, and the emergency revocation path.
objectives: Scalable, verifiable revocation using bitstring status lists
Staleness control via Δ with fail-closed/degrade policies
On-chain anchoring for tamper evidence (hash + timestamp anchor)

implementation_guidance.overview: The revocation mechanism operates as follows: Discovery maintains Bitstring Status Lists as signed JWT-VCs served over HTTPS. Each VC references a statusListIndex in a specific statusListCredential. Producer Sentinels cache status lists and verify them using on-chain hash anchors. The Δ parameter bounds how stale a cached status list can be before enforcement mode changes.
steps: Step 1 - Bitstring Status List Credential Structure: A StatusListCredential is a JWT-VC with type ['VerifiableCredential','BitstringStatusListCredential']. JWT payload: iss (Discovery DID), sub (same URL as status list ID), jti (UUID v4), iat, exp (iat + list_validity_seconds, default 1 day), vc.type as above, vc.credentialSubject.id (URL), vc.credentialSubject.type 'BitstringStatusList', vc.credentialSubject.statusPurpose ('revocation'|'suspension'), vc.credentialSubject.encodedList (base64url of gzip-compressed bitstring). The bitstring has one bit per credential index. Bit = 1 means revoked.

Step 2 - StatusListCredential naming: Use URL pattern https://discovery.example.gov/api/v1/status/{status_list_id} where status_list_id is a UUID. Each issuer and credential type combo gets its own status list (to limit blast radius). Maximum credentials per status list: 131072 (2^17 bits = 16KB compressed).

Step 3 - VC Status Reference: Each VC (specifically AccessGrantCredential) includes a credentialStatus claim: {id: '<status_list_url>#<index>', type: 'BitstringStatusListEntry', statusListIndex: '<integer_as_string>', statusListCredential: '<status_list_url>', statusPurpose: 'revocation'}. The index is assigned at VC issuance time and stored in the DB.

Step 4 - Status List Serve Endpoint: GET /api/v1/status/{status_list_id} is PUBLIC (no auth required). Returns the StatusListCredential JWT (content-type: application/vc+jwt). HTTP cache headers: Cache-Control: public, max-age={delta_seconds}, must-revalidate. Last-Modified and ETag based on last update timestamp.

Step 5 - Delta (Δ) Definition and Configuration: Δ is the maximum age of the cached status list anchor before Producer Sentinels must enter stale mode. Configuration values by environment: prod_delta_seconds=600 (10 min), test_delta_seconds=1800 (30 min), dev_delta_seconds=3600 (1 hour). These values are stored in TrustPolicyRegistry on-chain (TASK-015) and also in the Sentinel config bundle. On-chain values take precedence.

Step 6 - Staleness Mode Behavior: When Producer Sentinel detects stale status (now - last_anchor_updatedAt > Δ): Mode FAIL_CLOSED (default) — reject ALL requests with 503 STATUS_STALE_FAIL_CLOSED; Mode FAIL_OPEN_DEGRADED — allow only requests with scope limited to read-only operations; Mode ALLOW_WITH_WARNING — allow all but log warning + increment metric status_stale_requests_total. Mode is configured per environment in config bundle. Default is FAIL_CLOSED for prod.

Step 7 - On-Chain Anchoring: After Discovery publishes a new status list, it writes to StatusRegistry contract (TASK-016): publishStatusAnchor(statusListId=keccak256(url), rootHash=SHA256(jwt_bytes), updatedAt=block.timestamp_override_or_now). Sentinel reads the anchor: get_status_anchor(statusListId) → {rootHash, updatedAt, issuerId}. Sentinel verifies: SHA256(downloaded_status_list_jwt) == anchor.rootHash. If mismatch → reject as TAMPERED.

Step 8 - Emergency Revocation Path: For compromised high-value credentials, Discovery can call StatusRegistry.emergencyRevoke(credentialIdHash=keccak256(jti)) directly on-chain. Sentinels check this mapping on every VC verification: is_emergency_revoked(credentialIdHash) → bool. This path bypasses the status list. Cost: one on-chain write per credential. Restricted to security-admin role.

Step 9 - Cache Architecture at Sentinel: Sentinel maintains: (1) in-memory cache of decoded status list bitstrings, indexed by statusListId, (2) persistent cache copy in encrypted local storage for restart recovery, (3) last_anchor {rootHash, updatedAt} per statusListId cached from chain for freshness comparison. Refresh scheduler runs at Δ/2 interval with ±10% jitter to prevent thundering herd.

Step 10 - Status Verification Procedure (at Sentinel for each VC): (1) Extract credentialStatus.statusListCredential URL and statusListIndex. (2) Derive statusListId = keccak256(URL). (3) Check in-memory cache freshness: if cache_age > Δ → trigger refresh and get_anchor. (4) Get anchor from chain client cache (TASK-042). (5) If anchor.updatedAt > cached_download_time → download fresh status list. (6) Verify downloaded status list JWT signature. (7) Verify SHA256(jwt_bytes) == anchor.rootHash. (8) Decompress bitstring, check bit at statusListIndex. (9) If bit == 1 → return STATUS_REVOKED. (10) Check emergency revoke for credentialIdHash. (11) Return NOT_REVOKED.

files: libs/common/revocation/cache.py — StatusListCache with TTL and disk persistence

fields: url: str — the HTTPS URL where this is served
issuer_did: str — the DID of the issuer (Discovery)
credential_count: int — total capacity of this list
jwt_bytes: bytes — the raw signed JWT for hash verification

StatusListCache.description: Sentinel-side cache entry for a status list
fields: root_hash: str — SHA256 hex of jwt_bytes
anchor_updated_at: int — Unix timestamp from on-chain anchor
is_stale: bool — computed: now - anchor_updated_at > delta

revocation_check_errors.description: Credential bitstring bit is set to revoked
Status list is stale beyond Δ and mode is FAIL_CLOSED
SHA256 of downloaded status list does not match on-chain anchor
statusListIndex is outside the bitstring size
Status list URL unreachable and cache is stale

security_constraints: Status list download MUST verify the JWT signature before trusting content
Hash mismatch between downloaded status list and on-chain anchor MUST cause immediate rejection with TAMPERED code
Emergency revoke check MUST happen on every VC verification — cannot be cached beyond the anchor TTL
FAIL_CLOSED is the mandatory default for prod environments — FAIL_OPEN modes require explicit configuration with documented risk acceptance
Status list rollback (serving an older list) is detected by comparing anchor.updatedAt monotonically — if anchor shows newer timestamp but served list is older → TAMPERED

technical_requirements: HTTPS for distribution with proper cache headers
acceptance_criteria: Bitstring encode/decode/check utilities are implemented and tested
StatusListCache with TTL enforcement is implemented
Staleness detection (now - anchor_updated_at > Δ) is implemented and triggers configured mode
FAIL_CLOSED mode rejects all requests when status is stale
Hash mismatch between downloaded status list and on-chain anchor causes rejection
Emergency revoke path is implemented and tested
Refresh scheduler runs at Δ/2 with jitter
